text: Apple Inc. Form 10-K excerpt for fiscal 2023. Net sales were $383.3 billion in 2023 compared with $394.3 billion in 2022. Net income was $97.0 billion. Services net sales increased year over year and reached $85.2 billion. The company describes risks from supply chain disruption, competition, and dependence on third-party manufacturing and logistics.

Microsoft Corporation Form 10-K excerpt for fiscal 2023. Revenue was $211.9 billion, an increase from the prior year. Intelligent Cloud revenue was $87.9 billion and includes Azure and other cloud services, server products, and enterprise services. Operating income was $88.5 billion. The company highlights cybersecurity, cloud service reliability, and competition as important risk factors.

NVIDIA Corporation Form 10-K excerpt for fiscal 2024. Revenue was $60.9 billion, up 126 percent from the prior fiscal year. Data Center revenue was $47.5 billion, driven by demand for accelerated computing and generative AI infrastructure. Gross margin increased, and the company identifies supply constraints, customer concentration, and export controls as business risks.

Tesla, Inc. Form 10-K excerpt for fiscal 2023. Automotive revenue was the largest revenue category. The company discusses risks related to manufacturing scale, battery supply, raw material price volatility, product defects, regulatory requirements, and the need to expand charging and service infrastructure. Tesla also reports energy generation and storage revenue growth.

JPMorgan Chase & Co. Form 10-K excerpt for fiscal 2023. Net income was $49.6 billion and managed revenue was $162.4 billion. The firm discusses credit risk, market risk, liquidity risk, operational risk, cybersecurity risk, and regulatory capital requirements. Consumer and Community Banking, Corporate and Investment Bank, Commercial Banking, and Asset and Wealth Management are operating segments.
